channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Upwork unveils new capabilities for its Uma AI platform
item.description: On Wednesday, work marketplace Upwork introduced a host of new AI capabilities involving Uma, what it calls its “mindful artificial intelligence” tool. Uma is used to cut down time spent on listing jobs and helps match candidates with opportunities that are well suited for them.
item.dc:creator: Sadiya Kherani, Fast Company

item.title: Breaking down government hacks: The rise of the modern kill chain
item.description: This year, expect to see a record number of cyberattacks on government agencies as hackers ramp up their efforts ahead of the 2024 U.S. presidential election. The post Breaking down government hacks: The rise of the modern kill chain first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple MacBook Pro M4 leaks have exploded recently – and another heavy hint has just dropped that the laptop is about to arrive
item.description: Another rumor suggests Apple’s MacBook Pro M4 is imminent – and if it doesn’t arrive shortly, we can officially declare the rumor mill is useless.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Foot Locker hace de la tecnología una capacidad empresarial
item.description: El comercio minorista es dinámico, está en constante cambio y genera una gran cantidad de datos; y a través de la comercialización, las transacciones en tienda, la cadena de suministro, la tecnología digital y la fijación de precios, las oportunidades para aprovechar los datos son infinitas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: iOS 18: Maximize your privacy by turning off these iPhone settings now
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals some of the iPhone settings you may want to turn off to improve privacy after updating to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here’s how Cleary Gottlieb law firm uses genAI for pre-trial discovery and more
item.description: Corporate law is nothing like you see on television. To prepare for a case, 150 attorneys might be tasked to travel to remote warehouses to comb through tens of millions of documents gathering dust or track down amorphous electronic communications. It’s a process known as discovery.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cloud-enabled government: Seamless & secure data access
item.description: How is cloud adoption transforming federal agencies and driving mission success? The post Cloud-enabled government: Seamless & secure data access first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Why you shouldn’t store your money in payment apps
item.description: As use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: How AI can unlock innovation across our nation’s research institutions
item.description: At a university campus in Rolla, Missouri (population 22,000), a small team of scientists is developing a glass powder that, when applied to wounds, significantly improves blood clotting and reduces risk of infection.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Leland, Fast Company

item.title: EVs have a ‘Republican problem’ with divide deepening ahead of election
item.description: Even a recent influx of appearances from Tesla Inc.’s Elon Musk alongside Trump hasn’t helped reverse decades of derision against plug-in vehicles and the former president’s pledge to end Joe Biden’s EV policies on “day one.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Army modernization from the technology point of view
item.description: "We're working with our development teams and we're working with testing. And then we have a policy that covers all of that," said Young Bang. The post Army modernization from the technology point of view first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Derace Lauderdale, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal Trade Commission Adopts Final Rule for Click-to-Cancel Subscriptions
item.description: FTC adopts rule requiring businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI isn’t really that smart yet, Apple researchers warn
item.description: While we wait for the Age Of Apple Intelligence, it may be worth considering a recent Apple research study that exposes critical weaknesses in existing artificial intelligence models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 15 new Android 15 features to find on your Google Pixel phone
item.description: Oh, hello Android 15. How’s that for an all-around excellent October surprise? Yes, indeedly: Google’s latest and greatest Android version is officially on its way to Pixel devices near and far this week, just over a month after Google dropped the source code in an unusually detached move.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk unveils Tesla's Robovan, Robotaxi, humanoid robots
item.description: Tesla's "We, Robot" event reveals the Robovan, Robotaxi and the Optimus humanoid robot. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson examines the company's latest innovations in autonomous technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: About that brawl between the WordPress co-founder and WP Engine…
item.description: I’ve been a happy WordPress user for almost 20 years. Before that, I wrote HTML by hand or used content management systems such as Drupal to run my websites. WordPress was so much better that I never looked back — and I’m far from alone.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 5 ways to stop Windows Update from rebooting your PC
item.description: Windows Update can be a pain. Rebooting for updates is one thing — but a forced reboot for updates that shut down your running applications when you’re trying to get work done? Now, that’s obnoxious. Windows Update hit rock bottom in the early years of Windows 10.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Get started with the free-threaded build of Python 3.13
item.description: The single biggest new feature in Python 3.13 is something Python users have anticipated for ages: a version of Python that allows full concurrency between Python threads by removing the Global Interpreter Lock.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How Microsoft became a Big Tech choirboy
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Department of Justice have aggressively targeted Big Tech, suing Meta, Google, Amazon, and Apple for antitrust violations. And it’s not doing so in a small way — it’s filed multiple law suits against Google, for example.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Spotify is adding new markets to its music video test
item.description: Spotify is expanding its beta test of adding music videos to the platform by several dozen regions, though it still is unavailable in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Pressure grows for countries to deliver on promised biodiversity targets at UN conference
item.description: Countries will gather next week to assess whether they are close to reaching a global biodiversity goal of protecting 30% of land and water by 2030, known as 30 by 30.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey And Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NCSC seeks to expand counterintelligence outreach
item.description: A new national strategy warns that counterintelligence pros have to work more closely with other agencies and the private sector to address threats. The post NCSC seeks to expand counterintelligence outreach first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Apple powers up the iPad mini for Apple Intelligence
item.description: As expected, Apple has introduced a much faster Apple Intelligence-capable iPad mini equipped with the same A17 Pro chip used in the iPhone 15 Pro series. That’s a good improvement from the A15 Bionic in the previous model, and makes for faster graphics, computation, and AI calculation.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hide and lock your apps on your iPhone with iOS 18
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the iOS 18’s new features to hide and lock apps for better privacy and organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At the mercy of social media
item.description: I like Instagram as much as the next guy. I check in daily on my carefully curated feed of folks I follow and then work hard not to doomscroll the day away. One of my favorite follows is an adorable Corgi (aren’t they all adorable?) named Parsnip.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Open source package entry points could be used for command jacking
item.description: Open source application packages, including those in Python and JavaScript, have a vulnerability in their entry points that could be used by threat actors to execute malicious code to steal data, plant malware, and more.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: Making generative AI work for you
item.description: It’s not often someone can talk about genAI in a “pragmatic and realistic” way, but those are exactly the accolades handed out to AWS Product Management Director Massimo Re Ferrè following his recent generative AI (genAI) talk. It’s not hard to find opposing sentiment.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Fake Jennifer Aniston bikini body ad
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Will your AI bot put citizens at risk?
item.description: Government agencies have an obligation to provide accurate information to citizens, and a bad bot can have both legal and moral implications. The post Will your AI bot put citizens at risk? first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: AMD boosts AI performance in new line of enterprise PC chips
item.description: AMD’s big Advancing AI event in San Francisco on Thursday underlined how quickly the microprocessor industry has pivoted to artificial intelligence (AI) as its main sales pitch.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft
item.description: The Army Corps of Engineers will complete its hybrid cloud implementation by the end of fiscal 2025. Dovarius Peoples, former Corps’ CIO, lays out where the org The post Army Corps of Engineers modernizes IT infrastructure through hybrid cloud Vertosoft first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Will we see northern lights, power grid impacts amid 'severe' geomagnetic storm?
item.description: Earth is being hit by a strong solar radiation storm event that will be followed by a geomagnetic storm, space weather officials say.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation
